It decomposes complex tasks into parallel sub-tasks executed by dynamically instantiated, domain-specific agents.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e2. dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGeneral Testing Details\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWe report results for Kimi K2.5 and DeepSeek-V3.2 with thinking mode enabled, Claude Opus 4.5 with extended thinking mode, GPT-5.2 with xhigh reasoning effort, and Gemini 3 Pro with a high thinking level. For vision benchmarks, we additionally report results for Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUnless otherwise specified, all Kimi K2.5 experiments were conducted with temperature = 1.0, top-p = 0.95, and a context length of 256k tokens.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBenchmarks without publicly available scores were re-evaluated under the same conditions used for Kimi K2.5 and are marked with an asterisk (*).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWe could not evaluate GPT-5.2 xhigh on all benchmarks due to service stability issues. For benchmarks that were not tested, we mark them as \"-\".\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eText and Reasoning\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eHLE, AIME 2025, HMMT 2025 (Feb), and GPQA-Diamond were evaluated with a maximum completion budget of 96k tokens.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eResults for AIME and HMMT are averaged over 32 runs (avg@32); GPQA-Diamond over 8 runs (avg@8).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFor HLE, we report scores on the full set (text \u0026amp; image). Kimi K2.5 scores 31.5 (text) and 21.3 (image) without tools, and 51.8 (text) and 39.8 (image) with tools. The DeepSeek-V3.2 score corresponds to its text-only subset (marked with †) . Hugging Face access was blocked to prevent potential data leakage. HLE with tools uses simple context management: once the context exceeds a threshold, only the latest round of tool messages is retained.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTool-Augmented / Agentic Search\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eKimi K2.5 was equipped with search, code-interpreter, and web-browsing tools for HLE with tools and all agentic search benchmarks.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eExcept for BrowseComp (where K2.5 and DeepSeek-V3.2 used the discard-all strategy), no context management was applied, and tasks exceeding the supported context length were directly counted as failed.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe test system prompts emphasize deep and proactive tool use, instructing models to reason carefully, leverage tools, and verify uncertain information. Full prompts will be provided in the technical report.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eResults for Seal-0 and WideSearch are averaged over four runs (avg@4).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eVision Benchmarks\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMax-tokens = 64k, averaged over three runs (avg@3).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eZeroBench (w/ tools) uses max-tokens-per-step = 24k and max-steps = 30 for multi-step reasoning.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMMMU-Pro follows the official protocol, preserving input order and prepending images.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGPT-5.2-xhigh had ~10% failure rate (no output despite 3 retries), treated as incorrect; reported scores likely underestimate true performance.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWorldVQA, a benchmark designed to evaluate atomic vision-centric world knowledge. Access WorldVQA at \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/MoonshotAI/WorldVQA\"\u003ehttps://github.com/MoonshotAI/WorldVQA\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOmniDocBench Score is computed as (1 − normalized Levenshtein distance) × 100, where a higher score denotes superior accuracy.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eCoding Tasks\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTerminal-Bench 2.0 scores were obtained with the default agent framework (Terminus-2) and the provided JSON parser. In our implementation, we evaluated Terminal-Bench 2.0 under non-thinking mode. This choice was made because our current context management strategy for the thinking mode is incompatible with Terminus-2.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFor the SWE-Bench series of evaluations (including verified, multilingual, and pro), we used an internally developed evaluation framework. This framework includes a minimal set of tools—bash tool, createfile tool, insert tool, view tool, strreplace tool, and submit tool—along with tailored system prompts designed for the tasks. The highest scores were achieved under non-thinking mode.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe score of Claude Opus 4.5 on CyberGym is reported under the non-thinking setting.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAll reported scores of coding tasks are averaged over 5 independent runs.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLong-Context Benchmarks\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAA-LCR: scores averaged over three runs (avg@3).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLongBench-V2: identical prompts and input contexts standardized to ~128k tokens.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAgent Swarm\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBrowseComp (Swarm Mode): main agent max 15 steps; sub-agents max 100 steps.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWideSearch (Swarm Mode): main and sub-agents max 100 steps.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e4. response = client.chat.completions.create(\n        model=model_name, messages=messages, stream=False, max_tokens=4096\n    )\n    print('====== Below is reasoning_content in Thinking Mode ======')\n <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1. Model Introduction</h2><a id="user-content-1-model-introduction" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1. Model Introduction" href="#1-model-introduction"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Kimi K2.5 is an open-source, native multimodal agentic model built through continual pretraining on approximately 15 trillion mixed visual and text tokens atop Kimi-K2-Base. It seamlessly integrates vision and language understanding with advanced agentic capabilities, instant and thinking modes, as well as conversational and agentic paradigms.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Key Features</h3><a id="user-content-key-features" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Key Features" href="#key-features"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Native Multimodality</strong>: Pre-trained on vision–language tokens, K2.5 excels in visual knowledge, cross-modal reasoning, and agentic tool use grounded in visual inputs.</li>
<li><strong>Coding with Vision</strong>: K2.5 generates code from visual specifications (UI designs, video workflows) and autonomously orchestrates tools for visual data processing.</li>
<li><strong>Agent Swarm</strong>: K2.5 transitions from single-agent scaling to a self-directed, coordinated swarm-like execution scheme. It decomposes complex tasks into parallel sub-tasks executed by dynamically instantiated, domain-specific agents.</li>
</ul>

<details>
<summary><b>Footnotes</b></summary>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>General Testing Details
<ul dir="auto">
<li>We report results for Kimi K2.5 and DeepSeek-V3.2 with thinking mode enabled, Claude Opus 4.5 with extended thinking mode, GPT-5.2 with xhigh reasoning effort, and Gemini 3 Pro with a high thinking level. For vision benchmarks, we additionally report results for Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Thinking.</li>
<li>Unless otherwise specified, all Kimi K2.5 experiments were conducted with temperature = 1.0, top-p = 0.95, and a context length of 256k tokens.</li>
<li>Benchmarks without publicly available scores were re-evaluated under the same conditions used for Kimi K2.5 and are marked with an asterisk (*).</li>
<li>We could not evaluate GPT-5.2 xhigh on all benchmarks due to service stability issues. For benchmarks that were not tested, we mark them as "-".</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Text and Reasoning
<ul dir="auto">
<li>HLE, AIME 2025, HMMT 2025 (Feb), and GPQA-Diamond were evaluated with a maximum completion budget of 96k tokens.</li>
<li>Results for AIME and HMMT are averaged over 32 runs (avg@32); GPQA-Diamond over 8 runs (avg@8).</li>
<li>For HLE, we report scores on the full set (text & image). Kimi K2.5 scores 31.5 (text) and 21.3 (image) without tools, and 51.8 (text) and 39.8 (image) with tools. The DeepSeek-V3.2 score corresponds to its text-only subset (marked with †) . Hugging Face access was blocked to prevent potential data leakage. HLE with tools uses simple context management: once the context exceeds a threshold, only the latest round of tool messages is retained.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Tool-Augmented / Agentic Search
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Kimi K2.5 was equipped with search, code-interpreter, and web-browsing tools for HLE with tools and all agentic search benchmarks.</li>
<li>Except for BrowseComp (where K2.5 and DeepSeek-V3.2 used the discard-all strategy), no context management was applied, and tasks exceeding the supported context length were directly counted as failed.</li>
<li>The test system prompts emphasize deep and proactive tool use, instructing models to reason carefully, leverage tools, and verify uncertain information. Full prompts will be provided in the technical report.</li>
<li>Results for Seal-0 and WideSearch are averaged over four runs (avg@4).</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Vision Benchmarks
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Max-tokens = 64k, averaged over three runs (avg@3).</li>
<li>ZeroBench (w/ tools) uses max-tokens-per-step = 24k and max-steps = 30 for multi-step reasoning.</li>
<li>MMMU-Pro follows the official protocol, preserving input order and prepending images.</li>
<li>GPT-5.2-xhigh had ~10% failure rate (no output despite 3 retries), treated as incorrect; reported scores likely underestimate true performance.</li>
<li>WorldVQA, a benchmark designed to evaluate atomic vision-centric world knowledge. Access WorldVQA at <a href="https://github.com/MoonshotAI/WorldVQA">https://github.com/MoonshotAI/WorldVQA</a>.</li>
<li>OmniDocBench Score is computed as (1 − normalized Levenshtein distance) × 100, where a higher score denotes superior accuracy.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Coding Tasks
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Terminal-Bench 2.0 scores were obtained with the default agent framework (Terminus-2) and the provided JSON parser. In our implementation, we evaluated Terminal-Bench 2.0 under non-thinking mode. This choice was made because our current context management strategy for the thinking mode is incompatible with Terminus-2.</li>
<li>For the SWE-Bench series of evaluations (including verified, multilingual, and pro), we used an internally developed evaluation framework. This framework includes a minimal set of tools—bash tool, createfile tool, insert tool, view tool, strreplace tool, and submit tool—along with tailored system prompts designed for the tasks. The highest scores were achieved under non-thinking mode.</li>
<li>The score of Claude Opus 4.5 on CyberGym is reported under the non-thinking setting.</li>
<li>All reported scores of coding tasks are averaged over 5 independent runs.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Long-Context Benchmarks
<ul dir="auto">
<li>AA-LCR: scores averaged over three runs (avg@3).</li>
<li>LongBench-V2: identical prompts and input contexts standardized to ~128k tokens.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Agent Swarm
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BrowseComp (Swarm Mode): main agent max 15 steps; sub-agents max 100 steps.</li>
<li>WideSearch (Swarm Mode): main and sub-agents max 100 steps.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
</details>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">4. Native INT4 Quantization</h2><a id="user-content-4-native-int4-quantization" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 4. Native INT4 Quantization" href="#4-native-int4-quantization"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Kimi-K2.5 adopts the same native int4 quantization method as <a href="https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2-Thinking#4-native-int4-quantization" rel="nofollow">Kimi-K2-Thinking</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">5. Deployment</h2><a id="user-content-5-deployment" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 5. Deployment" href="#5-deployment"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p><p dir="auto">You can access Kimi-K2.5's API on <a href="https://platform.moonshot.ai" rel="nofollow">https://platform.moonshot.ai</a> and we provide OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible API for you. To verify the deployment is correct, we also provide the  <a href="https://kimi.com/blog/kimi-vendor-verifier.html" rel="nofollow">Kimi Vendor Verifier</a>.
Currently, Kimi-K2.5 is recommended to run on the following inference engines:</p>
</div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>vLLM</li>
<li>SGLang</li>
<li>KTransformers</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">The minimum version requirement for <code>transformers</code> is <code>4.57.1</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">Deployment examples can be found in the <a href="/MoonshotAI/Kimi-K2.5/blob/master/docs/deploy_guidance.md">Model Deployment Guide</a>.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">6. Model Usage</h2><a id="user-content-6-model-usage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 6. Model Usage" href="#6-model-usage"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The usage demos below demonstrate how to call our official API.</p>
<p dir="auto">For third-party APIs deployed with vLLM or SGLang, please note that:</p>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">Chat with video content is an experimental feature and is only supported in our official API for now.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">The recommended <code>temperature</code> will be <code>1.0</code> for Thinking mode and <code>0.6</code> for Instant mode.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">The recommended <code>top_p</code> is <code>0.95</code>.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">To use instant mode, you need to pass <code>{'chat_template_kwargs': {"thinking": False}}</code> in <code>extra_body</code>.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Chat Completion</h3><a id="user-content-chat-completion" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Chat Completion" href="#chat-completion"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This is a simple chat completion script which shows how to call K2.5 API in Thinking and Instant modes.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">openai</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">base64</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">requests</span>
<span class="pl-k">def</span> <span class="pl-en">simple_chat</span>(<span class="pl-s1">client</span>: <span class="pl-s1">openai</span>.<span class="pl-c1">OpenAI</span>, <span class="pl-s1">model_name</span>: <span class="pl-smi">str</span>):
    <span class="pl-s1">messages</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> [
        {<span class="pl-s">'role'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'system'</span>, <span class="pl-s">'content'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'You are Kimi, an AI assistant created by Moonshot AI.'</span>},
        {
            <span class="pl-s">'role'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'user'</span>,
            <span class="pl-s">'content'</span>: [
                {<span class="pl-s">'type'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'text'</span>, <span class="pl-s">'text'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'which one is bigger, 9.11 or 9.9? think carefully.'</span>}
            ],
        },
    ]
    <span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">client</span>.<span class="pl-c1">chat</span>.<span class="pl-c1">completions</span>.<span class="pl-c1">create</span>(
        <span class="pl-s1">model</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">model_name</span>, <span class="pl-s1">messages</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">messages</span>, <span class="pl-s1">stream</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">False</span>, <span class="pl-s1">max_tokens</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">4096</span>
    )
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">'====== Below is reasoning_content in Thinking Mode ======'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f'reasoning content: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">reasoning_content</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">'====== Below is response in Thinking Mode ======'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f'response: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">content</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>)

    <span class="pl-c"># To use instant mode, pass {"thinking" = {"type":"disabled"}}</span>
    <span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">client</span>.<span class="pl-c1">chat</span>.<span class="pl-c1">completions</span>.<span class="pl-c1">create</span>(
        <span class="pl-s1">model</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">model_name</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">messages</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">messages</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">stream</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">False</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">max_tokens</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">4096</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">extra_body</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span>{<span class="pl-s">'thinking'</span>: {<span class="pl-s">'type'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'disabled'</span>}},  <span class="pl-c"># this is for official API</span>
        <span class="pl-c"># extra_body= {'chat_template_kwargs': {"thinking": False}}  # this is for vLLM/SGLang</span>
    )
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">'====== Below is response in Instant Mode ======'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f'response: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">content</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>)</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="import openai
import base64
import requests
def simple_chat(client: openai.OpenAI, model_name: str):
    messages = [
        {'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are Kimi, an AI assistant created by Moonshot AI.'},
        {
            'role': 'user',
            'content': [
                {'type': 'text', 'text': 'which one is bigger, 9.11 or 9.9? think carefully.'}
            ],
        },
    ]
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model=model_name, messages=messages, stream=False, max_tokens=4096
    )
    print('====== Below is reasoning_content in Thinking Mode ======')
    print(f'reasoning content: {response.choices[0].message.reasoning_content}')
    print('====== Below is response in Thinking Mode ======')
    print(f'response: {response.choices[0].message.content}')

    # To use instant mode, pass {"thinking" = {"type":"disabled"}}
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model=model_name,
        messages=messages,
        stream=False,

        extra_body={'thinking': {'type': 'disabled'}},  # this is for official API
        # extra_body= {'chat_template_kwargs': {"thinking": False}}  # this is for vLLM/SGLang
    )
    print('====== Below is response in Instant Mode ======')
    print(f'response: {response.choices[0].message.content}')" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Chat Completion with visual content</h3><a id="user-content-chat-completion-with-visual-content" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Chat Completion with visual content" href="#chat-completion-with-visual-content"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">K2.5 supports Image and Video input.</p>
<p dir="auto">The following example demonstrates how to call K2.5 API with image input:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">openai</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">base64</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">requests</span>

<span class="pl-k">def</span> <span class="pl-en">chat_with_image</span>(<span class="pl-s1">client</span>: <span class="pl-s1">openai</span>.<span class="pl-c1">OpenAI</span>, <span class="pl-s1">model_name</span>: <span class="pl-smi">str</span>):
    <span class="pl-s1">url</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">'https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5/resolve/main/figures/kimi-logo.png'</span>
    <span class="pl-s1">image_base64</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">base64</span>.<span class="pl-c1">b64encode</span>(<span class="pl-s1">requests</span>.<span class="pl-c1">get</span>(<span class="pl-s1">url</span>).<span class="pl-c1">content</span>).<span class="pl-c1">decode</span>()
    <span class="pl-s1">messages</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> [
        {
            <span class="pl-s">'role'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'user'</span>,
            <span class="pl-s">'content'</span>: [
                {<span class="pl-s">'type'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'text'</span>, <span class="pl-s">'text'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'Describe this image in detail.'</span>},
                {
                    <span class="pl-s">'type'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'image_url'</span>,
                    <span class="pl-s">'image_url'</span>: {<span class="pl-s">'url'</span>: <span class="pl-s">f'data:image/png;base64, <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">image_base64</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>},
                },
            ],
        }
    ]

    <span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">client</span>.<span class="pl-c1">chat</span>.<span class="pl-c1">completions</span>.<span class="pl-c1">create</span>(
        <span class="pl-s1">model</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">model_name</span>, <span class="pl-s1">messages</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">messages</span>, <span class="pl-s1">stream</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">False</span>, <span class="pl-s1">max_tokens</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">8192</span>
    )
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">'====== Below is reasoning_content in Thinking Mode ======'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f'reasoning content: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">reasoning_content</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">'====== Below is response in Thinking Mode ======'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f'response: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">content</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>)

    <span class="pl-c"># Also support instant mode if you pass {"thinking" = {"type":"disabled"}}</span>
    <span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">client</span>.<span class="pl-c1">chat</span>.<span class="pl-c1">completions</span>.<span class="pl-c1">create</span>(
        <span class="pl-s1">model</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">model_name</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">messages</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">messages</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">stream</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">False</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">max_tokens</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">4096</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">extra_body</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span>{<span class="pl-s">'thinking'</span>: {<span class="pl-s">'type'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'disabled'</span>}},  <span class="pl-c"># this is for official API</span>
        <span class="pl-c"># extra_body= {'chat_template_kwargs': {"thinking": False}}  # this is for vLLM/SGLang</span>
    )
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">'====== Below is response in Instant Mode ======'</span>)
    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f'response: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">content</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>)

    <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">content</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="import openai
import base64
import requests

def chat_with_image(client: openai.OpenAI, model_name: str):
    url = 'https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5/resolve/main/figures/kimi-logo.png'
    image_base64 = base64.b64encode(requests.get(url).content).decode()
    messages = [
        {
            'role': 'user',
            'content': [
                {'type': 'text', 'text': 'Describe this image in detail.'},
                {
                    'type': 'image_url',
                    'image_url': {'url': f'data:image/png;base64, {image_base64}'},
                },
            ],
        }
    ]

    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model=model_name, messages=messages, stream=False, max_tokens=8192
    )
    print('====== Below is reasoning_content in Thinking Mode ======')
    print(f'reasoning content: {response.choices[0].message.reasoning_content}')
    print('====== Below is response in Thinking Mode ======')
    print(f'response: {response.choices[0].message.content}')

    # Also support instant mode if you pass {"thinking" = {"type":"disabled"}}
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model=model_name,
        messages=messages,
        stream=False,
        max_tokens=4096,
        extra_body={'thinking': {'type': 'disabled'}},  # this is for official API
        # extra_body= {'chat_template_kwargs': {"thinking": False}}  # this is for vLLM/SGLang
    )
    print('====== Below is response in Instant Mode ======')
    print(f'response: {response.choices[0].message.content}')

    return response.choices[0].message.content" tabindex="0" role="button">

    <span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f'response: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span>.<span class="pl-c1">choices</span>[<span class="pl-c1">0</span>].<span class="pl-c1">message</span>.<span class="pl-c1">content</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>'</span>)

    <span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">client</span>.<span class="pl-c1">chat</span>.<span class="pl-c1">completions</span>.<span class="pl-c1">create</span>(

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Interleaved Thinking and Multi-Step Tool Call</h3><a id="user-content-interleaved-thinking-and-multi-step-tool-call" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Interleaved Thinking and Multi-Step Tool Call" href="#interleaved-thinking-and-multi-step-tool-call"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">K2.5 shares the same design of Interleaved Thinking and Multi-Step Tool Call as K2 Thinking. For usage example, please refer to the <a href="https://platform.moonshot.ai/docs/guide/use-kimi-k2-thinking-model#complete-example" rel="nofollow">K2 Thinking documentation</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Coding Agent Framework</h3><a id="user-content-coding-agent-framework" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Coding Agent Framework" href="#coding-agent-framework"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
